question: An `application.yaml` still carries `camunda.client.zeebe.max-message-size=4MB`. To which Camunda 8.8 property should it be renamed?

a.text: The deprecation table maps `camunda.client.zeebe.max-message-size` to `camunda.client.max-message-size` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_MAXMESSAGESIZE`). Transport sizing moves up to the top-level client namespace.

b.text: There is no renamed `message-size-max` property; the rename only drops the `zeebe.` segment from the path.

c.text: `worker.defaults.*` covers job worker defaults (autocomplete, fetch variables, timeouts), not transport-level message size.

d.text: There is no `camunda.client.rest.max-message-size` property in the reference; the size limit is configured once on the client, not per protocol.

explanation: Transport-level settings from the deprecated `camunda.client.zeebe` namespace — `max-message-size`, `max-metadata-size`, `keep-alive`, `request-timeout`, `override-authority` — are promoted to the top-level `camunda.client.*` namespace as part of the unified Camunda 8 client.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
